methods.name: __init__
methods.description: Initializes event with task metadata: ID, arrival time, deadline, estimated execution time, resource requirement, and business priority level

methods.name: __init__
methods.description: Creates server instance with unique ID and empty job queue

methods.name: process
methods.signature: (env, event, log_store, agent=None, state=None, action=None, queue=None, servers=None)
methods.description: SimPy process that executes an event on the server with random service time, logs completion, and optionally updates Q-Learning agent with reward signal

methods.name: __init__
methods.description: Initializes routing engine with load balancer, optional rearrangement policy, optional RL agent, and operational mode (baseline/threshold/rl)

methods.name: run
methods.description: Main event routing loop handling: load prediction, auto-scaling (with cooldown), queue rearrangement on overload, and server selection via balancer or RL agent

methods.name: __init__
methods.description: Sets up SimPy simulation environment with specified event arrival rate and routing strategy

methods.name: event_producer
methods.description: Generator that creates events with exponential inter-arrival times and random task attributes (deadline, exec time, resource, priority)

methods.name: run
methods.description: Executes simulation until SIM_TIME, tracks queue depth history, returns (logs, remaining_queue, queue_history) tuple

methods.name: select_server
methods.description: Abstract method for selecting target server for event

methods.name: select_server
methods.description: Returns server with minimum job queue length using min() with lambda key function

methods.name: __init__
methods.description: Initializes linear regression predictor with choice of Linear/Ridge/Lasso and regularization strength

methods.name: engineer_features
methods.description: Extracts 13 temporal and statistical features: hour sin/cos, day-of-week sin/cos, weekday flag, holiday flag, day-of-month, queue depth, resource utilization, moving averages (5 and 15 periods), queue volatility

methods.name: create_training_data
methods.description: Engineers features, splits data into train/test sets, returns X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test with feature names

methods.name: train
methods.description: Trains model on data, scales features, computes train/test RMSE/MAE/R² metrics, stores training statistics

methods.name: predict_servers_needed
methods.description: Predicts optimal server count for given time and load conditions, clamps result to [1, 100] range

methods.name: predict_batch
methods.description: Batch prediction for multiple rows, applies feature engineering and model scaling

methods.name: get_feature_importance
methods.description: Returns model coefficients as feature importance dictionary

methods.name: __init__
methods.description: Initializes by loading pre-trained RPS and Server models from disk using default Windows paths

methods.name: _load_models
methods.description: Loads both RPS and Server pickle models, handles errors gracefully, sets is_loaded flag

methods.name: _load_pickle_file
methods.description: Loads pickle file with fallback strategies: tries joblib first, then standard pickle, then latin1 encoding

methods.name: get_temporal_features
methods.description: Extracts 5 temporal features for YEARLY model: hour (0-23), day_of_year (1-365), day_of_week (0-6), is_weekend (0-1), is_holiday (0-1)

methods.name: predict_rps
methods.description: Two-stage: extract temporal features → predict RPS (requests per second) using pre-trained model, ensures positive output

methods.name: predict_servers
methods.description: Predicts servers needed for given RPS using pre-trained Server model, rounds up to minimum 1

methods.name: predict_full_pipeline
methods.description: Complete 2-stage prediction pipeline: temporal features → RPS → Servers, returns dict with confidence estimate and statistics

methods.name: _update_statistics
methods.description: Updates running statistics: average RPS/servers, maximum RPS/servers, prediction count

methods.name: rearrange
methods.description: Abstract method for queue rearrangement policy

methods.name: __init__
methods.description: Initializes with priority weight configuration dictionary

methods.name: rearrange
methods.description: Updates event waiting times, when queue depth exceeds proactive threshold (FIXED_THRESHOLD - 2), sorts by weighted priority score

methods.name: __init__
methods.description: Initializes policy with spike detection threshold and normalized priority weights

methods.name: detect_spike
methods.description: Detects spike state using either ratio-based threshold (queue/servers) or prediction callback, tracks spike transitions

methods.name: calculate_priority_score
methods.description: Computes priority combining deadline urgency (0-1), business priority (0-1), and resource efficiency (0-1) with mode-dependent weighting

methods.name: rearrange_queue
methods.description: During spikes: sorts queue by priority score (descending); during normal periods: maintains FIFO order

methods.name: get_next_task
methods.description: Returns (next_event, was_rearranged) tuple with optional spike-triggered rearrangement

methods.name: __init__
methods.description: Initializes Q-table, sets alpha=0.1, gamma=0.9, epsilon=0.2

methods.name: get_state
methods.description: Discretizes queue length and server imbalance into state tuples

methods.name: choose_action
methods.description: Epsilon-greedy action selection: 0=use least-loaded balancer, 1=random server

methods.name: update
methods.description: Q-learning value update: Q(s,a) += α[r + γ*max(Q(s',a')) - Q(s,a)]

methods.name: __init__
methods.description: Initializes with learning parameters and defaultdict Q-table for 2 actions (ADD, REMOVE)

methods.name: get_state
methods.description: Maps queue length to 5-state buckets: 0=empty, 1=low(1-5), 2=medium(6-10), 3=high(11-20), 4=very high(21+)

methods.name: choose_action
methods.description: Epsilon-greedy selection: 0=ADD_SERVER, 1=REMOVE_SERVER

methods.name: update_q_value
methods.description: Standard Q-learning update rule with learning rate α and discount γ

methods.name: __init__
methods.description: Initializes multi-dimensional Q-Learning agent with 4 discrete action space and epsilon decay

methods.name: discretize_state
methods.description: Converts continuous values to discrete buckets: load(0-9), queue(0-9), servers(0-9), spike(0-3) = 10x10x10x4 state space

methods.name: get_state
methods.description: Creates state tuple by discretizing all input dimensions

methods.name: choose_action
methods.description: Epsilon-greedy selection from 4 actions: 0=add_server, 1=remove_server, 2=rearrange_queue, 3=do_nothing

methods.name: update_q_value
methods.description: Q-learning update with terminal state handling: Q(s,a) += α[r + γ*max(Q(s',a')) - Q(s,a)]

methods.name: calculate_reward
methods.description: Multi-factor reward: minimizes queue(-0.5), latency(-RT/100), over-provisioning(-2 if util<30%), SLA compliance(+5 if met), action-specific bonuses/penalties

methods.name: train_step
methods.description: Single training iteration for agent

methods.name: __init__
methods.description: Initializes ensemble orchestrator combining linear model predictions, Q-Learning decisions, and load prediction signals

methods.name: predict_future_load
methods.description: Predicts system load N minutes into future using time-based temporal patterns

methods.name: get_ensemble_prediction
methods.description: Combines 3 prediction methods: linear model (50%), current servers (30%), spike probability (20%) with confidence calculation

methods.name: __init__
methods.description: Initializes orchestrator using pre-trained integrated predictor (RPS → Servers models)

methods.name: get_temporal_state
methods.description: Returns temporal features for current predictions

methods.name: predict_future_rps
methods.description: Predicts RPS at future time N minutes from now

methods.name: predict_future_servers
methods.description: Predicts servers needed N minutes ahead using full pipeline

methods.name: get_decision
methods.description: Makes proactive decision comparing current vs future predicted state with trend analysis and confidence calculation

methods.name: __init__
methods.description: Initializes multi-scale orchestrator with predictor and policy

methods.name: generate_daily_plan
methods.description: Generates 24-hour capacity forecast with peak identification, spike windows, and operational recommendations (call at 6 AM)

methods.name: _identify_spike_windows
methods.description: Finds consecutive hours of peak demand (3+ consecutive peaks)

methods.name: _generate_daily_recommendations
methods.description: Creates operational actions (pre-scale timing, server counts, reasoning messages)

methods.name: hourly_checkpoint
methods.description: Hourly decision point for next-hour predictions and recommendations

methods.name: __init__
methods.description: Initializes simple moving average predictor with configurable window size

methods.name: moving_average
methods.description: Calculates moving average of queue lengths over window, returns 0 if empty

methods.name: add_measurement
methods.description: Records new queue length measurement to history

methods.name: predict
methods.description: Returns current load prediction (moving average of recent measurements)

methods.name: is_overloaded
methods.description: Returns True if predicted load exceeds specified threshold

methods.name: __init__
methods.description: Initializes with moving average window and spike detection threshold, includes time-of-day and day-of-week multipliers

methods.name: add_measurement
methods.description: Records timestamped load measurement for pattern analysis

methods.name: moving_average
methods.description: Calculates moving average of recent queue lengths

methods.name: get_baseline_load
methods.description: Returns baseline load from extended historical average

methods.name: get_time_factor
methods.description: Returns demand multiplier for hour of day: night=0.3, morning=0.8, business=1.2, evening=0.9, late=0.4

methods.name: get_day_factor
methods.description: Returns demand multiplier by day: weekday=normal, weekend=0.5, holiday=0.3

methods.name: predict_load
methods.description: Predicts load using baseline * time_factor * day_factor

methods.name: detect_spike
methods.description: Detects anomalous spikes: returns (is_spike, magnitude) where spike_magnitude > 1.5x baseline

methods.name: predict_spike_probability
methods.description: Predicts spike probability in next N minutes based on recent spike frequency

functions.name: compute_priority
functions.description: Weighted priority calculation: deadline_weight*(1/deadline) + exec_weight*exec_time + wait_weight*wait_time + resource_weight*resource + business_weight*priority + 0.05*wait_time(starvation prevention)

functions.name: step_1_generate_simulation_data
functions.description: Generates 30-day realistic simulation with holidays and demand spikes, exports metrics to CSV and summary JSON, prints statistics

functions.name: main
functions.description: Comprehensive experiment runner: tests 3 modes × 8 arrival rates × multiple weight sets, collects metrics (response time, p99, throughput, crash flags), exports to results/final_comparison.csv

functions.name: print_section
functions.description: Prints formatted section header with title

functions.name: test_model_loading
functions.description: Verifies RPS and Server models load correctly from disk, checks is_loaded flag

functions.name: test_basic_prediction
functions.description: Tests 2-stage prediction pipeline with example timestamp, shows RPS and server predictions with confidence

methods.name: __init__
methods.description: Initializes realistic simulation with configured holidays list, base demand, spike parameters

methods.name: add_holidays
methods.description: Registers custom holiday dates for demand reduction

methods.name: is_holiday
methods.description: Checks if timestamp falls on registered holiday

methods.name: get_time_of_day_multiplier
methods.description: Returns demand multiplier: morning=0.6, business=1.3, evening=1.0, night=0.4

methods.name: get_day_of_week_multiplier
methods.description: Returns day-based multiplier: weekdays=normal, Friday=0.85, weekend={0.5}

methods.name: run_simulation
methods.description: Executes month-long event-driven simulation collecting load and response time metrics

methods.name: export_metrics_to_csv
methods.description: Exports collected metrics to CSV file for analysis

methods.name: get_summary
methods.description: Returns simulation statistics dict with days, metric count, queue depths, spike events, holidays

functions.name: compare_decision
functions.description: Compares SimpleQLearningAgent vs EnhancedQLearningAgent decisions for scenario: shows state, action, confidence, all action scores
